body:
## Summary
- add `podcast overlays` command for timestamp-named PNG overlays
- ground overlay timestamps on the project/sequence timeline, then translate to the correct primary storyline item local timeline
- create FCPXML still-image resources and connected video clips with frame-snapped durations
- add optional opacity fade-in via `--fade-in`
- accept `.fcpxml`, `.fcpxmld` bundle, or `.fcpxmld/Info.fcpxml` inputs
- avoid FCP importer crashes by using video story elements for generated still overlays and leaving new media uids unset
- give generated imports a fresh project UID/name based on the output file
- document the workflow and add tests for timestamp parsing/FCPXML insertion

## Test
- `PYTHONPATH=src python -m pytest -q`
- `xmllint --noout --dtdvalid /tmp/FCPXMLv1_11.dtd timeline_overlays.fcpxml`
- manual generation: `PYTHONPATH=src python -c 'from podcast.cli import cli; cli()' overlays main-snapshot.fcpxmld overlay --duration 4.5 --fade-in 0.5 -o timeline_overlays.fcpxml`

comments.body:
## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |

| N/A | N/A | 0 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |

| — | — | not applicable | N/A | No rubrics configured for this repository |

## Notes

- No active rubrics are configured for `augmented-mind/podcast-editing-cli`. The rubrics directory was empty (only `.gitkeep` stubs exist on `agent/memory`).
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** All 4 tests pass (`PYTHONPATH=src python -m pytest -q` on `feature/overlay-fcpxml`). The implementation covers timestamp parsing, PNG dimension reading, FCPXML insertion with DTD-order compliance (connected clips before `<marker>`), lane collision avoidance, and `.fcpxmld` bundle resolution.
- **INFO:** `detect_frame_duration` in `fcpxml.py` does not guard against `sequence is None` before calling `.get("format")` — a `NoneType` AttributeError would surface on malformed FCPXML with no `<sequence>`. Low severity; existing tests use well-formed XML.
- **INFO:** `_next_resource_id` iterates every element in the tree for each overlay inserted (O(n×m)); negligible for typical timelines but could be pre-computed.

## Final Rubric Verdict

N/A

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

Both inline comments posted. Here is the synthesized review:

---

## Summary of PR/Issue

PR #2 adds a `podcast overlays` command that inserts timestamped PNG stills as connected clips into an FCPXML timeline. Overlays are discovered by parsing `M_SS` / `H_MM_SS` filename stems, read for dimensions via their IHDR chunk, and written as FCPXML `<asset>` + `<asset-clip>` pairs in the correct DTD order. The command accepts `.fcpxml`, `.fcpxmld`, or bare `Info.fcpxml` inputs. Four tests cover timestamp parsing, PNG dimension reading, FCPXML insertion, and bundle resolution.

## Review

The implementation is well-scoped, follows all existing project conventions, and handles FCPXML DTD ordering correctly. Two items deserve attention before merging: the asset `uid` is a file URI instead of a UUID, and the timestamp coordinate frame is source-media time (which silently misbehaves for trimmed clips and is undocumented).

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Timestamp coordinates are source-media time, not playback time | WARNING | `container_start` (the clip's trim `start`) is added to overlay timestamps; for trimmed clips this silently places overlays at wrong positions. At minimum must be documented; ideally reanchored to playback in-point. |
| `asset uid` is a file URI, not a UUID | WARNING | FCP expects a UUID for `uid`; using the file URI is non-standard and could confuse round-trip imports or tooling. `str(uuid.uuid4()).upper()` is the standard form. |
| Dead `or FRAME_DUR` fallback in `insert_overlays` | INFO | `detect_frame_duration` always returns a `Fraction` (never `None`), so the guard is unreachable and misleads future readers. |
| `_sequence_format` duplicates `detect_frame_duration` traversal | INFO | Both functions independently walk the tree to find the sequence format element; a minor DRY concern with no runtime impact. |
| Missing `hasAudio="0"` on still-image assets | INFO | FCP's own still-image assets always set `hasAudio="0"` explicitly; omitting it is harmless but non-standard. |
| `_next_resource_id` is O(n) per call, called O(n) times | INFO | O(n²) total; negligible for typical overlay counts (<100 PNGs) but trivially pre-computable. |
| `detect_frame_duration` does not guard `sequence is None` | INFO | A `NoneType` AttributeError would surface on malformed FCPXML with no `<sequence>`; low risk given well-formed inputs in practice. |

## Progress

- All 4 tests pass (`PYTHONPATH=src python -m pytest -q` on `feature/overlay-fcpxml`).
- DTD child-element ordering (connected clips before `<marker>`) is correctly implemented.
- Lane collision avoidance is in place.
- `.fcpxmld` bundle resolution is handled.
- The still-asset duration fix (matching timeline clip duration to avoid FCP importer crashes) is correct and well-commented.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Timestamp coordinates are source-media time, not playback time</summary>

**Cause:** `container_start = parse_time(container.get("start", "0s"))` reads the clip's source-media in-point (trim head). Adding `overlay.start` to it anchors timestamps to the raw source origin, not the visible playback start. For the common case of an un-trimmed clip (`start="0s"`) the result is correct and invisible. For a clip trimmed 10 minutes in, `0_05.png` correctly lands 5 s into the visible content, but `10_05.png` would land only 5 s in (605 s source) rather than 10 m 5 s into the visible clip.

**Candidate solutions:**
1. **Document only:** Clarify in the README and `insert_overlays` docstring that filenames are relative to the clip's source-media time origin.
2. **Reanchor:** Subtract `container_start` from the overlay timestamp so all filenames are relative to the playback in-point (`start = snap_to_frame(overlay.start, frame_dur)`), which is what most users will expect.

**Comments:** Option 2 is a one-line change and is likely more intuitive for users who think in "video playback time." The choice has product implications worth a quick maintainer decision.
</details>

<details>
<summary>`asset uid` is a file URI, not a UUID</summary>

**Cause:** `asset.set("uid", overlay.path.resolve().as_uri())` sets `uid` to a `file://` URI. The `uid` attribute is FCP's stable cross-project identity handle; FCP generates UUIDs (e.g., `F3A2B1C4-...`) for it.

**Candidate solutions:** Replace with `str(uuid.uuid4()).upper()`. The `src` attribute on `<media-rep>` already carries the file URI, so no information is lost.

**Comments:** Absolute paths are collision-free so round-trip import usually works, but it is semantically incorrect and could trip tools or FCP versions that validate the UUID format.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Dead `or FRAME_DUR` fallback in `insert_overlays`</summary>

**Cause:** `frame_dur = detect_frame_duration(root) or FRAME_DUR` — `detect_frame_duration` never returns `None` (it falls through to `return FRAME_DUR`), making the guard unreachable.

**Candidate solutions:** Remove the `or FRAME_DUR` suffix, or change `detect_frame_duration` to return `Fraction | None` and raise/log on missing format. Either clarifies intent.
</details>

<details>
<summary>Missing `hasAudio="0"` on still-image assets</summary>

**Cause:** `asset.set("hasVideo", "1")` is set but `hasAudio` is omitted. FCP-generated still-image assets always include `hasAudio="0"` explicitly.

**Candidate solutions:** Add `asset.set("hasAudio", "0")` after `hasVideo`. One-line fix.
</details>

## Recommended Next Step

`FIX_PR` — The two WARNING findings (UUID for `uid`, and either documenting or reanchoring the timestamp coordinate frame) are concrete, one-to-two-line changes safe for an automated fix pass.

## Final Verdict

`MINOR_ISSUES`

## Action Items

- [ ] Replace `asset.set("uid", src)` with `asset.set("uid", str(uuid.uuid4()).upper())` in `_append_overlay_asset` (`src/podcast/overlays.py:215`)
- [ ] Clarify or reanchor timestamp coordinates: either document in README/docstring that filenames are relative to source-media time origin, or subtract `container_start` to anchor to playback in-point (`src/podcast/overlays.py:317`)
- [ ] Remove the dead `or FRAME_DUR` guard from `frame_dur = detect_frame_duration(root) or FRAME_DUR`
- [ ] Add `asset.set("hasAudio", "0")` after `hasVideo` in `_append_overlay_asset`

title: Add FCPXML PNG overlay insertion
